instruction: I have 4 Python files (math_ops.py, string_ops.py, list_ops.py, dict_ops.py) with 24 functions total, but none of them have type annotations. Please add correct type hints to all function parameters and return values using Python 3.10+ syntax. Use concrete generic types like list[int], dict[str, int] (not bare list or dict). For functions that may return None, use the union syntax int | None. Do NOT modify the function logic — only add type annotations. You can verify correctness with mypy --strict. The 4 files are independent so you can assign each to a separate agent for parallel processing.
